content.problemStatement:
Chef has a collection of numbers of candidates candidateNumberscandidateNumberscandidateNumbers and a number targetSumtargetSumtargetSum. Chef wants to find all unique combinations of these candidateNumberscandidateNumberscandidateNumbers where the sum equals the targetSumtargetSumtargetSum. 

Each candidateNumber can be used only once in each combination. Help Chef find all such unique combinations without any duplicates.

Chef needs your help to implement a function that returns all these unique combinations.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindCombinationSum2findCombinationSum2findCombinationSum2 - This function finds all unique combinations of candidateNumberscandidateNumberscandidateNumbers that sum up to the targetSumtargetSumtargetSum.

Parameters
candidateNumberscandidateNumberscandidateNumbers: A vector of integers representing the numbers of candidates available for combinations.
targetSumtargetSumtargetSum: The target integer sum for which combinations are to be found.
Return Value
Returns an array of array of integers.
Each inner array represents a unique combination of candidateNumberscandidateNumberscandidateNumbers that add up to targetSumtargetSumtargetSum.
The combinations are returned in no particular order, and duplicates are excluded.
Constraints
1≤candidateNumbers.length≤1001 \leq \text{candidateNumbers.length} \leq 1001≤candidateNumbers.length≤100
1≤candidateNumbers[i]≤501 \leq \text{candidateNumbers}[i] \leq 501≤candidateNumbers[i]≤50
1≤targetSum≤301 \leq \text{targetSum} \leq 301≤targetSum≤30

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each test case contains:

First line: integers representing Chef's candidateNumbers.
Second line: An integer targetSumtargetSumtargetSum — the target sum Chef wants to achieve.




Output Format

For each test case, print all unique combinations of candidateNumbers that sum up to targetSum.
The order of combinations and the order of integers within a combination do not matter.
If no combination is found, print an empty line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the input array [2, 5, 2, 1, 2] with target 5, the unique combinations that sum to 5 are [1, 2, 2] and [5].
[1, 2, 2] is formed by picking 1 and two 2's; [5] is a single element equal to the target.

Explanation:
For the first test case (target=6, candidates=[4,3,2,7,3,5]):
Combinations that sum to 6 are [2 4] (2+4=6), [3 3] (3+3=6), and [4 5] (4+5=9 no, ignore). Correct sums are [3 3] and [2 4].


For the second test case (target=9, candidates=[1,1,2,5,6,7,10]):
Combinations summing to 9 are [1 1 2 5] (1+1+2+5=9), [2 7] (1+1+7=9), [1 2 6] (1+2+6=9), and [2 7] (2+7=9). These are the output lines matching sum 9.

solution.timeComplexity: O(2^n) in the worst case, since each number can be chosen or not.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) for the recursion stack and temporary combination storage, plus space for storing results.
